verdict_reason: Both disagreements (kauśalam; ubhe sukṛta-duṣkṛte) carry full explanation paragraphs. The kauśalam entry is the verse's central interpretive question and the explanation correctly distinguishes Shankara's 'skilfulness — the attitude of the skilful, the wisdom of equanimity' from Ramanuja's 'skill in action — evenness possible through great ability' from Prabhupada/Mukundananda's modern 'art of work / art of working skillfully'. The explanation also names the lexical floor (kuśala = skilled, fit, well) and the engineering-layer constraint ('preserve as SKILL OF ACTING, not as effort or enthusiasm'). The ubhe sukṛta-duṣkṛte entry correctly preserves the surprising claim (good karma binds too) and ties it to both Advaita (purification + Knowledge) and Vishishtadvaita (devotional renunciation) readings. Both substantive. Pass.
